question_text: Two identical cars drive on the same wet road. Car A has smooth tyres with a low coefficient of friction of 0.4, while Car B has textured tyres with a coefficient of friction of 0.8. Both cars have a mass of 1000 kg. Calculate the maximum friction force each car can experience. Why would a driver prefer Car B, especially in wet conditions?
correct_answer: Normal force = 1000 kg × 10 N/kg = 10,000 N. Car A: friction = 0.4 × 10,000 N = 4,000 N. Car B: friction = 0.8 × 10,000 N = 8,000 N. Car B is safer because the textured tyres provide twice as much friction (8,000 N vs 4,000 N), allowing better grip, faster braking, and better control on wet roads.
explanation: Textured tyres have a higher coefficient of friction than smooth tyres. This greater friction helps the car brake faster and turn without skidding, making it much safer in wet conditions.
